growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.1 (closed system, no exogenous carbon/energy source, following wash from R2B growth medium)
growth_records.cite: Shoemaker et al. Microbial population dynamics and evolutionary outcomes under extreme energy limitation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2101691118. [PMC8379937]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Flavobacterium sp. KBS0721 ... T2 days 75
growth_records.notes: Table 1 T2 = 75 days under 1,000-d energy limitation. doubling_time_h = 75 x 24 = 1800 h; mu = ln2/1800 = 0.000385 h-1. Same conditions/method as other Table 1 entries in this paper.

growth_records.medium: defined P-rich/P-limited minimal medium (Tanner 2007) with glucose as sole organic C
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% mu_max for each isolate... Flavobacterium sp.-64 | Dilution rate (h-1) 0.05 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state => mu = D = 0.05 h-1 (25% mu_max => mu_max ~0.20 h-1). doubling_time = 0.693/0.05 = 13.86 h. No uptake/secretion rates measured (only biomass C, P and C:P_B). Medium recipe cited to Tanner (2007); glucose 27 mM C constant C source. pH not reported. TER(C:P)=162; H' 1.65 (P-sufficient) / 1.90 (P-deficient).

growth_records.medium: defined P-rich/P-limited minimal medium (Tanner 2007) with glucose as sole organic C
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% mu_max for each isolate... Flavobacterium sp.-51 | Dilution rate (h-1) 0.06 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state => mu = D = 0.06 h-1 (25% mu_max => mu_max ~0.24 h-1). doubling_time = 0.693/0.06 = 11.55 h. No uptake/secretion rates measured (only biomass C, P and C:P_B). Medium recipe cited to Tanner (2007); glucose 27 mM C constant C source. pH not reported. TER(C:P)=302; H' 1.40 (P-sufficient) / 14.7 (P-deficient, strong homeostasis).

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 141186 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/141186
